name: Cymatic Resonance
description: A resonant plate covered in glowing sand: the grains shimmer where the plate flexes and collect onto the nodal lines, forming a live Chladni figure. Loudness drives how hard the whole plate vibrates, a beat is a burst that makes the sand jump, brightness (spectral centroid) selects which mode/figure — and when it changes, grains sitting on the old lines are suddenly at antinodes so they scatter and re-collect into the new figure. Harmony rotates the jewel hue. ~400K sand grains do the vibration-driven random walk (Zhou et al. 2017; ported per FA #73) on the plus-basis eigenmode field, depositing onto a diffusing density map. CR.2 rebuild — the sand is the phenomenon, not the static figure.
author: Matt
